g.text: A raw continuous feature: Age (synthetic Titanic-flavoured, n=1500) — skewed, non-monotone
Carver — consecutive groupings ranked by Tschuprow's T (best first)
Stage 1 / 4 — Discretizers — split into clean ordered bins
QuantitativeDiscretizer splits Age into 11 ordered bins above min_freq. Categorical, ordinal
and datetime features get the same treatment.
Carver — consecutive groupings ranked by Tschuprow's T (best first)
Stage 2 / 4 — Carvers — rank every consecutive grouping
BinaryCarver tries every consecutive grouping into ≤ 5 buckets — 385 candidates ranked by
Tschuprow's T. Adjacent bins sharing a colour form one bucket.
Carver — consecutive groupings ranked by Tschuprow's T (best first)
The highest-T grouping that stays viable on a held-out dev set (min_freq + distinct target
rates) wins — gold cuts on the strip.
Carver — consecutive groupings ranked by Tschuprow's T (best first)
4 interpretable buckets capture the non-monotone age–survival risk — ready for scorecards, WOE
Carver — consecutive groupings ranked by Tschuprow's T (best first)
